channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.
channel.copyright: Copyright:(C) Wed, 17 Jun 2026 07:16:33 IST Bennett Coleman & Co. Ltd, http://in.indiatimes.com/policyterms/1554651.cms

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: "The procedures are getting to his brain": Clavicular thrashed online after claiming IShowSpeed gave Cristiano Ronaldo a career
item.description: Streamer Clavicular sparked backlash after claiming he only knows Cristiano Ronaldo because of IShowSpeed and suggesting Speed “gave” the football icon a career. The comments quickly went viral, prompting strong reactions from fans. Many argued that Ronaldo was already one of the most famous athletes in history long before Speed rose to prominence. While some acknowledged Speed’s influence among younger audiences, most users dismissed Clavicular’s remarks and defended Ronaldo’s global legacy.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: "Her job is to cry": Candace Owens tears into Erika Kirk over emotional on-air moment amid Charlie Kirk death questions
item.description: Candace Owens criticized Erika Kirk’s emotional on-air response while discussing the ongoing controversy surrounding Charlie Kirk’s death. Owens argued that public sympathy is repeatedly used to deflect questions about the case, describing Erika as an “emotional asset” and claiming her reactions have become predictable. The remarks sparked debate online, with some defending Erika’s grief and others supporting Owens’ call for continued scrutiny of unresolved questions surrounding Charlie Kirk’s death.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: Indian techie Sai Karthik Varma Datla among 12 people killed in Missouri plane crash
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: What would you do on a Saturday night in Boston? Former US diplomat says F-1 visa can be rejected by this one question
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: MrBeast’s $1M challenge winner Philip DeFranco plans massive giveaway for subscribers
item.description: YouTuber Philip DeFranco is gifting his entire $1 million MrBeast challenge prize to his subscribers. Forty lucky fans will each receive $25,000. DeFranco, who unexpectedly won the competition, revealed this generous plan in a TikTok video. He also alluded to potential attempts to influence voting during the challenge, though he remained unaware at the time.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Kai Cenat tells fans to stay home as Atlanta Streamer University event fails to secure venue
item.description: Kai Cenat's Streamer University application event in Atlanta has been postponed. Organizers cited venue issues due to overwhelming interest. Several locations withdrew last minute. Fans are advised not to travel. Atlanta remains the planned final stop. A new venue and date are being sought to ensure safety and manage the large expected turnout. Updates will be provided once confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Luigi Mangione trial: Hearing rescheduled after prosecutors fail to notify jail to bring him to court
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Inside Son Doong: The Vietnam cave bigger than a Boeing 747 that took 17 years to find again
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: ‘You can’t die here’: Why this French coastal town issued a bizarre death ban rule that stunned the world
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Don't Sharia Our Texas': Muslim GOP members asked to leave Republican convention, US if they don't become Christians
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: ‘Weapon more powerful than any nuke’: Iran can shut Strait of Hormuz again, US intel warns
item.description: Iran now possesses the capability to effectively shut down the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global oil and gas route, according to US intelligence. This development comes despite an upcoming peace agreement aimed at reopening the waterway. Concerns persist over Iran's military arsenal and its potential to use the strait as leverage in future disputes, even as diplomatic efforts continue.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Can always come to Moscow': Russia's open invitation to Zelenskyy for ‘serious’ dialogue
item.description: Ukraine's President Zelenskyy is welcome in Moscow for serious peace talks, the Kremlin stated, though no official communication channels exist. This comes amid renewed diplomatic efforts, including US President Trump's discussions with Zelenskyy and anticipated visits by American representatives to Russia. European leaders urged a firmer stance against Moscow at the G7 summit.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: US used Iran-style covert transfers to move 90 million barrels of oil out of Gulf: Report
item.description: The U.S. has reportedly established a covert offshore oil-transfer network near the Strait of Hormuz, mirroring Iran's tactics to maintain Gulf crude exports. This operation, involving ship-to-ship transfers off Oman and the UAE, aims to circumvent Tehran's blockade of the vital waterway. While enabling continued exports, the network's reliance on clandestine methods raises concerns about its vulnerability and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Donald Trump's White House ballroom cost estimated at $600 million, taxpayers footing half the bill: Report
item.description: President Trump's ambitious White House ballroom project is now reportedly costing a staggering $600 million, with over half expected to be funded by taxpayers, contradicting earlier claims of private financing. Despite assurances of a "tax-payer free" initiative, internal plans reveal significant public funds allocated to the construction, sparking controversy and legal challenges.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: How tall is Vice President JD Vance? His reply to surprised hosts of 'The View' as he walks in the studio
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: I am Austin Franco but not the racist one: Dallas attorney receives threat over 'unfortunate coincidence', says 'he even looks like me'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Land of the free kick: For Iran, it's 90mins of football and six hours of border crossing
item.description: Iran's football team drew 2-2 with New Zealand in Los Angeles amidst significant logistical challenges and political undertones. Players faced immediate travel to Mexico post-match, with coach Ghalenoei criticizing the lack of recovery time. The tournament has seen multiple teams complain about stringent security and restrictive measures, impacting the athlete experience.
item.dc:creator: Chidanand Rajghatta

item.title: Boating incident in Sydney turns out to be murder-suicide: Note found after Indian-origin man dies along with daughter
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'We're not trying to be philanthropists': Australian couple who donated more than $100 million to charity say it's not a big deal
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Attacks stopped cold': FBI says it thwarted 'potential threat' to White House UFC event
item.description: The FBI announced the thwarting of an alleged plot targeting the UFC America 250 event at the White House. Multiple suspects were taken into custody following a multi-state operation after authorities became aware of the threat days before the event. Law enforcement agencies are credited with preventing planned attacks.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
